explanation: The natural language statement "If A, then B" implies a global implication where A is "currentADCSMode is equal to NMC" and B is "control_error shall be greater than or equal to 10". The LTL formula captures this by stating that 'Globally' (G), if 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC' is true, then 'control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10' must also be true. This ensures that the condition holds at every point in time.

The natural language statement describes a condition that must always hold: if a specific ADCS mode is active, then a certain error condition must be met. The 'If A, then B' structure translates to an implication (A -> B). The phrase 'shall be' typically indicates a global requirement, meaning this implication must hold true at all times. Therefore, the LTL formula uses the Global (G) operator to ensure that the implication `currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC -> control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10` is always true.

The natural language statement 'If A, then B' is directly translated into the logical implication 'A -> B'. The phrase 'shall be' implies that this condition must hold true at all times in the future. Therefore, the global operator 'G' is used to assert that the implication 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC -> control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10' must always be true.

The natural language statement specifies a conditional relationship that must hold at all times: 'If A, then B'. In LTL, a property that must always be true is expressed using the global operator 'G'. The 'If ... then ...' structure is captured by the implication operator '->'. Therefore, the LTL formula G(currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC -> control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10) states that at every point in time (G), if 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC' is true, then it must immediately follow that 'control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10' is also true.

The statement "If P, then Q" translates to the logical implication P -> Q. The phrase "shall be" implies that this condition must hold true at all times (globally). Therefore, if 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC' is true, it must always imply that 'control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10' is also true. This is captured by the global operator 'G' applied to the implication.

The natural language statement describes a global implication. 'If A, then B' translates to 'A -> B'. The phrase 'shall be' indicates that this condition must hold true at all times in the future (globally). Therefore, the LTL formula G(A -> B) captures this requirement, meaning 'Globally, if currentADCSMode is equal to NMC, then control_error must be greater than or equal to 10'.

The statement describes a conditional relationship that must always hold: if 'currentADCSMode is equal to NMC' is true, then 'control error is greater than or equal to 10' must also be true at the same time. The LTL operator 'G' (globally) ensures that this implication holds true at all points in time. The '->' operator represents the 'if-then' relationship.

The statement "If A, then B" translates to the implication A -> B. The phrase "shall be" implies that this condition must hold globally. Therefore, if 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC' is true at any point, then 'control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10' must also be true at that same point. This is captured by a global implication.

The 'G' operator ensures that this implication holds globally, i.e., at every moment in time. The '->' operator represents the 'if-then' relationship.

This means that at any point in time, if the condition "currentADCSMode is equal to NMC" (represented by `currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMC`) is met, then the condition "control error is greater than or equal to 10" (represented by `control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10`) must also be true at that same time. The 'shall be' indicates that this implication holds at all times, which is captured by the Global (G) operator.

This directly captures the 'If A, then B' structure of the natural language statement, with 'shall' indicating a global invariant.

The 'then' clause 'control_error shall be greater than or equal to 10' is represented by 'control_error_greaterThanOrEqual_10'. The phrase 'shall be' indicates that this condition must hold true globally, whenever the antecedent is met. Therefore, a global implication (G (A -> B)) is the correct translation, ensuring that at any point in time, if the 'currentADCSMode' is 'NMC', then 'control_error' must be greater than or equal to 10.
